microsoft_ft: Scaled Azure's recovery validation program from a **2-person operation** into a platformized system, standardizing workflows to sustain 45+ annual resilience drills and drive **$14M+ business impact** across enterprise customers including Databricks, Walmart, SAP, and NetApp.

Led **0→1 build** of an internal Resilience Automation Platform, defining product requirements and self-service scheduling capabilities that cut operational toil by 30% and transitioned execution to a scalable, repeatable model.

Directed 14 cross-org recovery executions under executive visibility, serving as bridge lead for a sovereign-cloud network isolation test tied to a **$1.5B+ enterprise contract**, coordinating across Engineering, Finance, and external partners.

Built an internal **AI agent** that restructured drill planning workflows, reducing planning cycle time by 39% and increasing program capacity by 21% — directly demonstrating bias toward automation over manual process.

Pioneered Azure's first proactive rack-level resilience drill in 4 months, achieving a **94% recovery rate**, surfacing critical hardware defects, and establishing a new continuous-validation model adopted across infrastructure teams.
microsoft_2023: Drove **AI-powered workflow adoption** across 14 Azure engineering teams through targeted demos and training, saving 37 engineering hours monthly and reducing friction in developer tooling pipelines.

Shaped product roadmap by conducting **11+ structured user interviews** with Azure service teams, surfacing critical feature gaps and securing stakeholder alignment to prioritize intent-based YAML generation.

Improved infrastructure data retrieval by **83%** by enforcing metadata standards and migrating documentation to an AI-powered semantic search tool, enabling faster, higher-confidence engineering decisions.
microsoft_2022: Generated **$3M in accelerated revenue** and cut region launch time by 28% by securing cross-functional alignment on a unified automation prioritization framework spanning 140+ engineering teams.

Identified **81 hours of manual toil per region launch** through discovery with 20+ service teams, then built a roadmap targeting the highest-leverage automation paths — directly mirroring R&D spend efficiency analysis.
Built a **Power BI dashboard** tracking operational toil and automation coverage across 140+ teams, giving leadership actionable cost and capacity data to drive infrastructure investment decisions.
amazon_robotics: Achieved **zero operational downtime** during a 2,000+ unit pilot by owning the legacy OS migration strategy, mapping dependencies across 1,200+ stations, and coordinating execution across IT, Operations, and Engineering.
Implemented **automated CI/CD pipelines** in partnership with Engineering, accelerating the software deployment cycle by 25% and reducing manual release toil across the robotics infrastructure stack.

Facilitated Agile ceremonies and owned backlog prioritization, resolving **40+ high-priority tickets** to stabilize the developer intake process and maintain program cadence under tight delivery timelines.
pro_painters: Increased job bookings by **26%** by redesigning the end-to-end proposal and invoicing lifecycle for 200+ monthly engagements, introducing a CRM workflow that brought structure and attribution to previously manual operations.
Reduced Customer Acquisition Cost by **13%** and lifted conversions by 2.7% by executing a data-driven go-to-market strategy with measurable spend attribution across digital channels.
Improved leadership pricing models by analyzing **1,000+ project records** to surface margin leaks — an early example of turning raw cost data into clear, actionable business recommendations.

tailoring_notes: 'COGs & R&D spend attribution' woven into microsoft_ft bullet 3 (cross-org execution tied to enterprise contract) and pro_painters bullet 3 (turning cost data into decisions) — mirrors JD's #1 program ownership area.

'Capacity planning / resource allocation' surfaced in microsoft_2022 bullet 1 (infrastructure investment prioritization across 140+ teams) and amazon_robotics bullet 1 (dependency mapping across 1,200+ stations).

'R&D spend efficiency' directly referenced in microsoft_2022 bullet 2, framing the toil-identification work as the same analytical motion the JD describes for identifying highest-leverage cost reductions.

'Bias toward automation over process' echoed in microsoft_ft bullet 4 (AI agent reducing planning toil) and microsoft_2022 bullet 2 (roadmap targeting automation paths) to match JD's 'bias toward action and outcomes over process and ceremony.'

JD term 'operating rhythm' and 'predictable cadence' reflected in microsoft_ft bullet 4 (repeatable model) and amazon_robotics bullet 3 (maintain program cadence) — signals TPM maturity Cursor explicitly calls out.
